daily_brief: Escalating hostilities between the US and Iran dominated headlines Wednesday, with Iranian drones striking Kuwait's international airport and killing one person, while US forces conducted retaliatory strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island and intercepted missiles targeting Bahrain and Kuwait [BBC World], [France 24], [Le Monde]. The OECD warned of a 'dark scenario' if the Gulf energy crisis persists [Financial Times]. Meanwhile, Russia launched one of its deadliest attacks on Ukraine in months, killing at least 22 civilians across Kyiv, Dnipro, and other cities with a barrage of 656 drones and 73 missiles [BBC Europe], [Globe and Mail]. Ukraine responded with drone strikes on St Petersburg's oil terminal during Putin's economic forum [ABC Australia]. In Europe, the EU unveiled an ambitious technology sovereignty strategy aimed at reducing dependence on US and Asian suppliers [RFI English], [Le Monde], while also reaching agreement on new migration rules permitting 'return hubs' outside the bloc [RFI English]. The Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda continues to generate international concern, with 60 deaths reported and controversy erupting over US plans for a quarantine facility in Kenya [The Guardian], [Le Monde].

top_stories.headline: US and Iran Exchange Strikes as Gulf Hostilities Intensify; Kuwait Airport Hit
bullets: Iranian drones struck Kuwait's international airport and other infrastructure, killing one person and injuring several others, according to Kuwait's foreign ministry
US Central Command said it conducted 'self-defence' strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island targeting a military ground control station, and intercepted Iranian missiles fired at Kuwait and Bahrain
Iran claimed it attacked US bases and helicopters in a 'regional country' using missiles and drones in retaliation
The escalation comes as US-Iran diplomatic talks show little sign of progress, with the OECD warning of economic consequences if the crisis continues

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed factual account emphasizing both sides' military actions

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage noting stalled peace talks

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article inaccessible but headline confirms US claims of thwarting attacks

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Focused on economic implications with OECD warning

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Massive Strike on Ukraine, Killing at Least 22 Civilians
bullets: Russia fired 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukrainian cities including Kyiv and Dnipro in one of its largest overnight assaults in months
At least 22 people were killed including children, with 16 deaths in Dnipro alone where residential buildings were struck
Ukraine's air force intercepted over 90% of drones and most cruise missiles but only 11 of 33 Iskander-M ballistic missiles
The Kremlin described the attacks as 'systematic strikes' in response to Ukrainian attacks, pledging continuation

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Emphasized civilian toll and included ground-level reporting from affected neighborhoods

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Contextualized strikes as part of pattern when Russian military is struggling

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Included Ukrainian drone counterattack on bus in Russian-controlled territory

top_stories.headline: EU Unveils Major Strategy to Reduce Dependence on US and Asian Technology
bullets: European Commission to announce plan targeting 'technological sovereignty' with investment in homegrown chips, cloud computing, and AI
Over 80% of the EU's digital products, services, and infrastructure currently come from foreign providers
Package includes new laws on cloud computing and AI to triple EU data center capacity within 5-7 years
Initiative risks tensions with Washington, which has opposed EU regulations targeting US tech firms

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of specific measures including chips law and data center plans

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article inaccessible but headline confirms focus on distancing from US and Asia

top_stories.headline: Ukraine Strikes St Petersburg Oil Terminal as NATO Chief Visits Kyiv
bullets: Ukrainian drones hit an oil export terminal in St Petersburg hours before Putin's annual economic forum began
Leningrad region governor reported 59 drones shot down overnight; airport temporarily restricted operations
President Zelensky confirmed strikes on the fuel terminal and a military facility near Russia's second-largest city
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte made an unannounced visit to Kyiv amid the escalating attacks

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Emphasized timing relative to Putin's economic showcase forum

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Covered within broader context of Russian strikes and counterclaims

top_stories.headline: Ebola Outbreak Declared International Emergency; Controversy Over US Quarantine Plan in Kenya
bullets: WHO declared the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of international concern, with 60 deaths and 344 confirmed cases in DRC, one death and nine cases in Uganda
Two people killed in Kenya during protests against US plan to establish an Ebola quarantine facility at Laikipia Air Base for American citizens
Nobel laureate Denis Mukwege warned the outbreak 'could become the deadliest ever'
DR Congo's football friendly against Chile in Spain cancelled by local authorities over health concerns

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Kenyan anger and accusations of double standards by US

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Reported on Spanish mayor canceling football match as precautionary measure

additional_stories.headline: Ghana passes sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activity with prison sentences of 3-10 years, sparking fear among community members
Five Mozambican nationals killed in xenophobic attacks in South Africa as anti-migrant protests sweep the country
Israel-Hezbollah partial ceasefire holds as Israel agrees not to bomb Beirut in exchange for Hezbollah not attacking Israel
EU reaches deal on migration rules allowing 'return hubs' outside the bloc for deportees while advancing Western Balkans enlargement
Japan's fertility rate hits record low for tenth consecutive year with only 671,236 births in 2025
US Supreme Court allows Alabama to use pro-Republican congressional map eliminating one majority-Black district
Fire at New Delhi hotel kills at least 21 people, including foreign medical tourists from African countries
France bans Israeli offensive weapons from Eurosatory defense exhibition, allowing only anti-ballistic and air defense systems
US Trade Representative recommends new tariffs of up to 12.5% on 60 economies including China, Japan, and South Korea over forced labor concerns
Hungary's new PM Peter Magyar meets Macron to discuss Ukraine support and European defense after defeating Viktor Orbán
